m_Item1.QuestionText: Three or more devices share a link in ________ connection.
m_Item1.Explanation: A multipoint connection is a link between three or more devices. It is also known as Multi-drop configuration. The networks having multipoint configuration are called Broadcast Networks. In broadcast network, a message or a packet sent by any machine is received by all other machines in a network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which topology covers security, robust and eliminating traffic factor?
m_Item1.Explanation: In case of Mesh topology, there are no data traffic issues as there is a dedicated link between two devices which means the link is only available for those two devices.Mesh is reliable and robust as failure of one link doesn’t affect other links and the communication between other devices on the network. Mesh is secure because there is a point to point link thus unauthorized access is not possible. Fault detection is easy.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Multipoint topology is ________.
m_Item1.Explanation: Mainly used in LANs . All the nodes are connected to a single cable.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A simple cabling method, known as the ………… topology, allows about 30 computers on a maximum cable length of about 600 feet.
If all devices are connected to a central hub, then topology is called  ________.
Which of the following is/ are the drawbacks of Ring Topology?

Options.OptionText: Failure of one computer, can affect the whole network.
Adding or removing the computers disturbs the network activity.
If the central hub fails, the whole network fails to operate.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Topology with heighest reliability is ________.
m_Item1.Explanation: In Mesh Topology each node is connected with every node in the network. So the data to be transmitted will find some or the other route in the network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is stat frame delimeter (SFD) in ethernet frame?
What is the size of MAC Address?
Layer-2 Switch is also called
A set of rules that governs data communication
The data link layer takes the packets from _________ and encapsulates them into frames for transmission.
What is CRC in cyclic redundancy checking?
m_Item1.Explanation: CRC is cyclic redundancy check. CRC is a code for error- detection. It is mostly used in digital networks and sometimes in storage devices to detect any change in the raw data.
